title: About Stony Brook University
clean_text: Stony Brook University (SBU) is a public research university located in Stony Brook, New York, on Long Island. Founded in 1957, SBU is part of the State University of New York (SUNY) system and is one of the flagship campuses. The university sits on a 1,454-acre campus and serves over 26,000 students. SBU is known for its strong programs in science, technology, engineering, mathematics, medicine, and the humanities. It is classified as an R1 doctoral university, meaning it conducts very high research activity. Stony Brook is consistently ranked among the top public universities in the United States and the world.

title: History of Stony Brook University
clean_text: Stony Brook University was founded in 1957 as State University College on Long Island, originally located in Oyster Bay. It moved to its current location in Stony Brook in 1962. The university has grown from a small teachers college to a major research institution. Nobel laureates, Turing Award winners, and leading researchers across disciplines have been affiliated with SBU. The university played a major role in the development of Long Island's tech and biomedical economy. SBU manages Brookhaven National Laboratory jointly with Battelle Memorial Institute under contract with the U.S. Department of Energy.

title: SBU Mascot - Wolfie the Seawolf
clean_text: Stony Brook University's mascot is Wolfie the Seawolf. The athletic teams are called the Seawolves. The Seawolves compete in NCAA Division I athletics and are members of the America East Conference for most sports. The name 'Seawolves' reflects the university's location on Long Island near the Atlantic Ocean and its strong marine sciences program. The official colors of Stony Brook University are red, grey (officially called 'seawolf grey'), and white.

title: SBU Rankings and Recognition
clean_text: Stony Brook University is consistently ranked among the top public universities in the United States. It is ranked in the top 100 universities worldwide by major rankings including QS World University Rankings and Times Higher Education. U.S. News & World Report ranks SBU among the best national universities and top public schools. SBU is a member of the Association of American Universities (AAU), an elite group of 71 leading research universities in the United States and Canada. The university has produced numerous Nobel Prize winners and National Academy of Sciences members among its faculty and alumni.

title: President of Stony Brook University
clean_text: Stony Brook University is led by President Maurie McInnis, who became the university's fifth president in 2020. President McInnis is an art historian and higher education leader with a focus on student success, research excellence, and community engagement. The Office of the President is located in the Administration Building on the main campus. The Provost is the chief academic officer of the university. For official university leadership information, visit stonybrook.edu/president.

title: SBU Location and Campus
clean_text: Stony Brook University is located in Stony Brook, New York, on the North Shore of Long Island. The main campus is approximately 60 miles east of New York City and accessible via the Long Island Rail Road (LIRR) from Penn Station or Grand Central Terminal (the Stony Brook station is on the Port Jefferson branch). The 1,454-acre main campus includes academic buildings, residence halls, dining facilities, a hospital, sports facilities, and research centers. There is also a Southampton campus focused on environmental sciences. The university hospital, Stony Brook Medicine, is one of the largest teaching hospitals in the region.

title: Colleges and Schools at SBU
clean_text: Stony Brook University is organized into several colleges and schools: College of Arts and Sciences, College of Engineering and Applied Sciences, College of Business, School of Medicine, School of Dental Medicine, School of Nursing, School of Health Technology and Management, School of Social Welfare, School of Marine and Atmospheric Sciences, College of Professional Education, and the Graduate School. The university offers over 200 undergraduate programs, 100+ master's programs, and 50+ doctoral programs.

title: SBU Research Excellence
clean_text: Stony Brook University is one of the nation's premier research institutions. It co-manages Brookhaven National Laboratory, a U.S. Department of Energy facility that has produced seven Nobel Prize winners. SBU's research portfolio spans physical sciences, life sciences, engineering, social sciences, and humanities. The university receives hundreds of millions of dollars in research funding annually from federal agencies, foundations, and industry. Key research areas include cancer biology, climate science, artificial intelligence, neuroscience, marine science, and materials science. The Institute for AI-Driven Discovery and Innovation (AI Institute) focuses on cutting-edge AI research.

title: About Ask Seawolves - SBU AI Assistant
clean_text: Ask Seawolves is the official AI-powered assistant for Stony Brook University. It is designed to help students, applicants, faculty, staff, and visitors find accurate information about SBU programs, services, policies, and resources. Ask Seawolves draws from official SBU web sources and provides cited answers grounded in university information. It can help with questions about admissions, tuition, housing, dining, financial aid, academic calendars, course registration, IT services, faculty, departments, career services, and much more. For personal account information (grades, financial aid status, enrollment), students should use SOLAR or contact the relevant office directly. Ask Seawolves is available 24/7 and routes complex or sensitive questions to the appropriate university office.

title: Notable SBU Alumni and Affiliates
clean_text: Stony Brook University has produced many distinguished alumni and has hosted world-renowned faculty. Notable alumni include Dario Amodei (CEO of Anthropic), various senior figures in technology, medicine, finance, and public service. The university's faculty and alumni include Nobel Prize laureates in Physics, Chemistry, and Medicine, as well as Turing Award winners. The Simons Foundation, founded by SBU mathematics faculty member Jim Simons, supports major research in mathematics, physics, and life sciences globally. SBU's connection to Brookhaven National Laboratory has enabled faculty involvement in major scientific discoveries.

title: SBU Campus Culture and Student Life
clean_text: Stony Brook University has a vibrant and diverse campus community. The student body represents all 50 states and more than 100 countries. SBU has over 300 student clubs and organizations, including academic, cultural, athletic, professional, and social groups. The campus hosts regular events, concerts, lectures, and cultural celebrations throughout the academic year. Notable traditions include Roth Pond Regatta (cardboard boat race), the Strawberry Festival, and various cultural festivals celebrating the university's diverse community. The Student Activities Center (SAC) is the hub of student life on campus.
